title: Filter vs Wrapper Methods
concept: Filter methods score features individually before modeling, whereas wrapper methods test different feature combinations by actually training models.

exercises.prompt: Warmup: Why do we drop columns with extremely low variance?

options.text: They are nearly constant and offer no pattern for the model to learn from
They cause the model to calculate numbers too quickly
They have too many different text categories inside them

exercises.explain: Spot on! If a column doesn't change, it has no predictive power. Now, how do we choose which features to keep?

cards.title: Auditioning a band
cards.body: If you're forming a band, you could listen to each musician play solo and rate them (fast and easy). Or, you could have different combinations of musicians play together to see who sounds best (slower, but finds the best chemistry). These are filter and wrapper methods.

cards.title: Filter vs. Wrapper
cards.body: Filter methods evaluate features individually based on statistical properties (like correlation or variance) before any model is trained. Wrapper methods search for the best combination of features by repeatedly training models on different subsets and scoring their performance.

cards.title: Filtering vs. Wrapping
cards.body: Think of Filter methods like checking height requirements for basketball players — you 'filter' out players below 6 feet instantly. Wrapper methods are like running practice games with different team lineups to see which combination wins.

cards.title: Speed vs. Accuracy
cards.body: If you have 10,000 features, a Filter method (like correlation) takes seconds because it's just math on the columns. A Wrapper method would have to train thousands of models, which could take hours or days, but might find a better result.

cards.title: Wrappers aren't always better
cards.body: While wrapper methods find features that work well together, they have a major catch: they are slow and highly prone to overfitting because they tailormake the feature list to fit a specific model's quirks.

cards.title: Filter vs. Wrapper
cards.body: Contrasting the two feature selection approaches:

left.text:
Fast statistical scoring
Ignores feature interactions
Independent of models

right.text:
Slow iterative search
Finds feature chemistry
Dependent on models

exercises.prompt: What is the key characteristic of filter methods in feature selection?

options.text: They score features based on statistics before training any models
They wrap the model in a security layer to protect data
They require training a model for every single feature
They only work on image and text datasets

exercises.explain: Filter methods assess features using statistical properties (like correlation) without running a machine learning algorithm.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Wrapper methods are generally faster than filter methods because they use machine learning models.
exercises.explain: False! Wrapper methods are much slower because they must train and evaluate models on many different feature combinations.

exercises.sentence: A feature selection method that tests different subsets of features by actually training models is called a ___ method.
exercises.explain: Wrapper methods 'wrap' the feature selection process around the model training step.

exercises.prompt: Which scenario describes a filter method?

options.text: Calculating the correlation of 100 columns and keeping the top 10
Training a random forest model 50 times with different combinations of columns
Manually asking domain experts to select columns they think are cool

exercises.explain: Calculating correlation is a statistical test independent of model training, making it a filter method.

exercises.prompt: You have a dataset with 5,000 features and need to build a model in 10 minutes. You want to select the best features quickly. Which approach should you take?

options.text: Run a filter method first to quickly drop thousands of low-signal features
Run a wrapper method on all 5,000 features to get the absolute best combination
Train the model on all 5,000 features without selecting any
Ask a colleague to manually read all 5,000 columns

exercises.explain: Outstanding! A filter method is extremely fast and can trim down the massive feature list in seconds, fitting your tight time limit perfectly. Next: putting it all together to achieve maximum feature density!
